title: OperationalDNA
description: A complete Operational DNA document for an application instance. Composes a domain hierarchy with the full behavior stack (capabilities, causes, rules, outcomes, equations) and the SOP stack (positions, persons, tasks, processes).

domain.description: The root domain, containing the full nested hierarchy of subdomains and nouns.

capabilities.description: All Noun:Verb capability pairs defined in this domain.

causes.description: What initiates each capability — user actions, schedules, webhooks, or chained capabilities.

rules.description: Constraints on capabilities — who may perform them (access rules) and what conditions must be satisfied (condition rules).

outcomes.description: State changes and side effects that result from each capability.

equations.description: Named, technology-agnostic computations that cannot be expressed as declarative Rules or Outcomes. Implemented concretely by Scripts in Technical DNA.

signals.description: Named domain events published after a Capability executes. Signals cross domain boundaries and carry typed payload contracts.

relationships.description: Named, directed connections between Nouns — formalizes the semantic link a reference Attribute implies, adding cardinality.

positions.description: Organizational job titles. Each Position carries Roles (declared in Product Core DNA) and is referenced by Tasks and Persons.

persons.description: Individuals who currently fill Positions — the business org roster. Documentation-grade DNA, not authentication identity.

tasks.description: Atomic reusable units of human activity: a Position performing exactly one Capability.

processes.description: Standard Operating Procedures — named, owned, ordered DAGs of Steps that accomplish business goals.
